seoTitle: How to Create a Team in Jira Cloud Easily
metaDescription: Learn how to create an Atlassian team in Jira, add members, set access permissions, and assign issues directly to your new team.
intro: When staff profiles are scattered across disconnected groups, it's hard to keep ownership clear across projects. This guide covers creating an Atlassian Team inside Jira, adding members, configuring access, and assigning issues to that team once it's set up.

steps.heading: Open the team creation menu
steps.body: From your Jira Cloud dashboard, use the Teams option in the main header menu and select Create a Team.

steps.heading: Name and describe your team
steps.body: Enter a team name, optionally upload an avatar, and write a short description of what the team is responsible for.

steps.heading: Add team members
steps.body: Use the Add People field to type in the names or emails of the people who should be part of the team.

steps.heading: Set access permissions
steps.body: Choose whether the team allows open joining or requires approval, depending on how tightly you want to control membership.

steps.heading: Assign issues to your team
steps.body: Open an existing issue, locate the Team field, and assign it to your newly created team so work can be tracked under that group.

faq.question: How do I create a team in Jira?
faq.answer: Open the Teams dropdown in the main navigation, select Create a Team, name it, add members, and click Create Team.

faq.question: Are Jira Teams shared across Confluence too?
faq.answer: Yes, Atlassian Teams are designed to maintain alignment across Jira, Confluence, and other Atlassian products under one directory.

faq.question: Can I control who joins my Jira team?
faq.answer: Yes, you can choose an open-join setting to let people join freely, or lock it down so membership requires approval.

faq.question: Can I assign a Jira issue to a team instead of an individual?
faq.answer: Yes, open the issue, find the Team field, and select your team to assign the work at the team level.
